title: [Bug] [Win32] Build Fails under Msys2, MinGW
author: samuelvenable
body:
### Operating System

Windows

### What's the issue you encountered?

This is the error in the terminal I get when attempting to build it using the regular means to do so from the documentation / readme. 

```
[534/577] Linking CXX shared library plugins\ui.hexpluglib
FAILED: [code=1] plugins/ui.hexpluglib plugins/ui/libui.dll.a
C:\WINDOWS\system32\cmd.exe /C "cd . && C:\msys64-imhex\mingw64\bin\c++.exe -O3 -DNDEBUG  -shared -fuse-ld=lld -Wa,mbig-obj -Wl,--wrap=_ZSt21__glibcxx_assert_failPKciS0_S0_ -o plugins\ui.hexpluglib -Wl,--out-implib,plugins\ui\libui.dll.a -Wl,--major-image-version,0,--minor-image-version,0 plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/library_ui.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/hex_editor.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/pattern_drawer.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/visualizer_drawer.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/menu_items.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/pattern_value_editor.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/widgets.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/markdown.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/editor.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/highlighter.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/navigate.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/render.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/support.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/utf8.cpp.obj plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/text_editor/codeFolder.cpp.obj -LC:/msys64-imhex/home/Windows/ImHex/lib/libimhex/../third_party/libdlgmod plugins/fonts/libfonts.dll.a  lib/libimhex/liblibimhex.dll.a  plugins/ui/libromfs/lib/libromfs-ui.a  lib/external/libwolv/libs/io/liblibwolv-io.a  lib/external/libwolv/libs/net/libwolv-net.a  -lWs2_32  lib/external/libwolv/libs/math_eval/libwolv-math_eval.a  lib/external/libwolv/libs/utils/libwolv-utils.a  -lntdll  -lgdiplus  -lcomctl32  -lshlwapi  -lcomdlg32  -lole32  -loleaut32  -luuid  C:/msys64-imhex/home/Windows/ImHex/lib/libimhex/../third_party/libdlgmod/libdlgmod.a  C:/msys64-imhex/mingw64/lib/libfreetype.dll.a  C:/msys64-imhex/mingw64/lib/libglfw3.dll.a  -lopengl32  -lglu32  C:/msys64-imhex/mingw64/lib/libcurl.dll.a  -LC:/msys64-imhex/mingw64/lib -lmbedtls -lmbedx509 -lmbedcrypto  lib/third_party/lunasvg/liblunasvg.a  lib/third_party/lunasvg/plutovg/libplutovg.a  -lm  lib/trace/libtracing.a  lib/third_party/llvm-demangle/libLLVMDemangle.a  -lDbgHelp  lib/third_party/md4c/src/libmd4c.a  -lkernel32 -luser32 -lgdi32 -lwinspool -lshell32 -lole32 -loleaut32 -luuid -lcomdlg32 -ladvapi32 && cd ."

ld.lld: error: undefined symbol: hex::fonts::Default()
>>> referenced by plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/markdown.cpp.obj:(hex::ui::Markdown::Markdown(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char>>)::'lambda0'(MD_BLOCKTYPE, void*, void*)::_FUN(MD_BLOCKTYPE, void*, void*))
>>> referenced by plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/markdown.cpp.obj:(hex::ui::Markdown::Markdown(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char>>)::'lambda0'(MD_SPANTYPE, void*, void*)::_FUN(MD_SPANTYPE, void*, void*))
>>> referenced by plugins/ui/CMakeFiles/ui.dir/source/ui/markdown.cpp.obj:(hex::ui::Markdown::Markdown(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char>>)::'lambda'(MD_SPANTYPE, void*, void*)::operator()(MD_SPANTYPE, void*, void*) const (.constprop.0.isra.0))

>>> referenced 7 more times
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
[541/577] Building CXX object plugins/visualizers/CMakeFiles/visualizers.dir/source/plugin_visualizers.cpp.obj
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
```

### How can the issue be reproduced?

build main branch from source as of 7/10/26

### ImHex Version

main branch

### ImHex Build Type

- [x] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

git clone

### Additional context?

my fork im testing this build with doesn't even modify the files it errored at, or anything related to it. the fork is identical to the upstream main branch. there's only slight modification to libimhex's cmakelists.txt and fs.cpp. the rest is the same and up to date, so it really can't be an issue with my fork

title: [Feature] Export virtual file system
author: Aurel300
body:
### What feature would you like to see?

I would like to be able to export the full file hierarchy I can see in the virtual filesystem (created using `hex::core::add_virtual_file`) to disk.

### How will this feature be useful to you and others?

When reversing archive formats (say the data files for a game), exporting the archive into separate files is useful for further inspection.

### Request Type

- [ ] I can provide a PoC for this feature or am willing to work on it myself and submit a PR

### Additional context?

_No response_

title: [Bug] Imhex does not create directories in %localappdata%
author: TBM13
body: ### Operating System Windows ### What's the issue you encountered? Imhex (standalone, installed using the MSI `imhex-1.39.0.WIP-Windows-x86_64.msi` installer) is failing to save its settings. Every time I open it, it thinks it is starting for the first time. The Help->About->Directories shows the config dir in red, just like it does with all other directories in `%localappdata%`: Upon looking, the `%localappdata%/imhex` directory does not exist. I had to manually create it and all its subdirectories for them to appear blue in imhex and fix the settings not saving. ### How can the issue be reproduced? 1- Install Imhex from the MSI installer 2- Launch Imhex, go through the first-launch screens and optionally change a setting. 3- Restart Imhex. It will once again show the first-launch screens and the setting changed in step 2 will have its original value ### ImHex Version 1.39.0.WIP ### ImHex Build Type - [x] Nightly or built from sources ### Installation type Latest MSI ### Additional context? _No response_

title: [Bug] [FreeBSD] `<sys/xattr.h>` header not found.
author: samuelvenable
body:
### Operating System

FreeBSD

### What's the issue you encountered?

According to a quick Google search, this is a Linux/macOS-specific header and therefore will not exist on FreeBSD. After further investigation the FreeBSD equivalent header is `<sys/extattr.h>` however it uses an incompatible API.

### How can the issue be reproduced?

Compile on FreeBSD.

### ImHex Version

master branch

### ImHex Build Type

- [x] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

git clone

### Additional context?

Error produced in `file_provider.cpp` under `plugins`.

Windows

ImHex crashes when a pattern recurses over ~345-350 times (`#pragma eval_depth` does not help)

title: [Feature] Pattern editor allow to read from external files
author: PerfectlyFineCode
body:
### What feature would you like to see?

Allow reading from external files in pattern editor for files that depend on each other during extraction, say an index and a packed data relations

### How will this feature be useful to you and others?

Currently have an index file which contains information about packed files in relative directory, they depend on each other. There is currently no way to reference external files, which means the pattern completely fails as I would have to individually open each packed file separately. Index file contains where in the packed data said information exists as well as the size of it

### Request Type

- [ ] I can provide a PoC for this feature or am willing to work on it myself and submit a PR

### Additional context?

_No response_

title: [Bug] No titlebar in linux build
author: codeman4033
body: ### Operating System Linux ### What's the issue you encountered? I launch imhex and immediately, I noticed that there's no titlebar. ### How can the issue be reproduced? You launch the app without "Use OS Window decorations" checked. ### ImHex Version v1.39.0.WIP; master@af55d25 ### ImHex Build Type - [x] Nightly or built from sources ### Installation type AppImage+Arch pkg.tar.zst ### Additional context? Sure, I fixed it by checking "Use OS Window decorations", but this shouldn't even be an issue to begin with.

title: Debugging On Windows ARM64 builds
author: shewitt-au
body:
### Operating System

Windows

### What's the issue you encountered?
When building a debug version on Windows (building on an ARM system targeting ARM, debugging with VSCode), debugging doesn't work properly. For example, breakpoints can't be set.

Form ARM64 builds clang is used. Clang's debugger (lldb-dap in my case) requites the correct DWARF debugging info, which isn't the default.

See this [PR](https://github.com/WerWolv/ImHex/pull/2769) for an example fix.

This works for me, but it might need to be cleaned up.

### How can the issue be reproduced?

Build an ARM64 (uses Clang) and try to debug. As mentioned, I'm VSCode.

### ImHex Version

v1.39.0.WIP-Debug

### ImHex Build Type

- [x] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

Built from source

title: [Bug] File larger than 2 GB with max RAM truncates the file
author: neptuwunium
body:
### Operating System

Linux

### What's the issue you encountered?

When opening a file with "Max file size to load into RAM" set to a number greater than 2 GB, the file is all zeroes past 0x7fffffff (INT_MAX).


### How can the issue be reproduced?

- Set Max file size to load into RAM to a value greater than 2 GB 
- Locate a file larger than  that amount GB, otherwise create one
- Navigate to offset 0x7fffffff
- Observe file being zeroes after 0x7fffffff

### ImHex Version

7f9f7289400029b0c6eceae373e215e5eb3ebaee

### ImHex Build Type

- [x] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

Manually Built

### Additional context?

_No response_

title: [Bug] Interacting with Hex Editor or opening Pattern Editor disables keyboard navigation
author: bbb651
body:
### Operating System

Linux

### What's the issue you encountered?

Keyboard navigation gets globally disabled when Interacting with Hex Editor or opening Pattern Editor, so pretty much always.

It's pretty straight forward why this happens, [keyboard navigation is turned on when initializing imgui
](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AWerWolv%2FImHex%20%22ImGuiConfigFlags_NavEnableKeyboard%22&type=code), then [calls to `discardNavigationRequests()`](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AWerWolv%2FImHex%20discardNavigationRequests&type=code) disable it and it's never enabled again.

### How can the issue be reproduced?

- Interact with Hex Editor or open Pattern Editor
- Keyboard navigation doesn't work in e.g. Find search results and Pattern Data

### ImHex Version

1.38.1

### ImHex Build Type

- [ ] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

ArchLinux AUR `imhex-bin` package

### Additional context?

_No response_

title: [Bug] EnterNotify doesn't reliably cause a redraw on i3
author: StomyPX
body:
### Operating System

Linux

### What's the issue you encountered?

I'm running i3 on Arch (for this, I doubt distro matters) and when I switch workspaces away from ImHex and then back to it, the window contents doesn't redraw until I interact with a UI element that would trigger a redraw, like mousing over a row in the data inspector.

I'm assuming ImHex is only drawing on relevant events, and it works fine most of the time. But when flipping back and forth between ImHex on one workspace and something else on another it has this annoying extra step of needing to fling the cursor around the screen to get it to redraw after bringing ImHex back up.

The window redraws properly if it loses focus again while remaining on-screen, so it does seem to handle the LeaveNotify XEvent properly but it's inconsistent regarding EnterNotify.

### How can the issue be reproduced?

Running with i3 window manager on a single monitor:

1. Open imhex on workspace 1
2. Position mouse so it is not hovering over any controls
3. Switch to workspace 2 (this will cause imhex's window contents to be abandoned by i3, which is expected behavior)
4. Switch back to workspace 1 again.
5. Instead of imhex you will see a still of the contents of workspace 2 until you move the cursor over a control that highlights on hover.

### ImHex Version

1.38.1

### ImHex Build Type

- [ ] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

package `imhex-bin` from Arch User Repository

### Additional context?

_No response_

When an ImHex window is open with a file and positioned behind the browser, cursor changes on hover over browser elements stop working correctly. For example, the cursor changes to the correct pointer, but a moment later it reverts back to the default.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Open ImHex 2. Select Language = Russian 3. Create new file 4. Go to the Edit menu 5. Hover your mouse over the "Paste" option Resulting in two menu items with the same visible label: ### ImHex Version 1.38.1 ### ImHex Build Type - [ ] Nightly or built from sources ### Installation type MSI ### Additional context? _No response_

title: [Feature] Dear ImGui File Selection Dialogs
author: samuelvenable
body: ### What feature would you like to see? Examples: - SDL3 Windowing / SDL3 Renderer Backend: https://github.com/samuelvenable/SDL3-ImGui-FileDialogs - SDL2 Windowing / SDL2 Renderer Backend: https://github.com/samuelvenable/SDL2-ImGui-FileDialogs - SDL2 Windowing / OpenGL 3.3 Renderer Backend: https://github.com/samuelvenable/ImFileDialog Windows: Macintosh: Ubuntu: ### How will this feature be useful to you and others? - The file selection dialogs do not stay-on-top of the parent window on Windows, (this would fix that issue). - Unified look-and-feel across platforms; this is the main reason; native dialogs can be set to stay-on-top too. ### Request Type - [ ] I can provide a PoC for this feature or am willing to work on it myself and submit a PR ### Additional context? In order to use the same Dear ImGui theme as the rest of your application, you need to do this before creating dialogs: ```cpp #include // default="-2", classic="-1", dark="0", light="1", custom="2"... ngs::fs::environment_set_variable("IMGUI_DIALOG_THEME", "0"); // dark ```

title: [Feature] [Pattern Language] Add the ability to check the length of a file.
author: GameLord2011
body:
### What feature would you like to see?

Add something to get the length of the file being inspected without using `std::file`.

### How will this feature be useful to you and others?

It would allow for easier footer validation with custom patterns.

### Request Type

- [ ] I can provide a PoC for this feature or am willing to work on it myself and submit a PR

### Additional context?

I made a file format that has a footer, and am making an ImHex pattern for debugging. It would be nice if there was a footer for it so that it is easier to check the footer.

title: [Bug] Data race in log::debug() when debug logging is not enabled
author: bthiae2934
body:
### Operating System

Windows

### What's the issue you encountered?

I found ImHex crashes when it starts. Here's the stack trace:

```
0:010> k
 # Child-SP          RetAddr               Call Site
00 0000001d`433fe3e8 00007ffb`93d89676     ntdll!NtWaitForMultipleObjects+0x14
01 0000001d`433fe3f0 00007ffb`93d892df     ntdll!WerpWaitForCrashReporting+0x82
02 0000001d`433fe470 00007ffb`93e0bcd8     ntdll!RtlReportExceptionHelper+0x4d3
03 0000001d`433fe5f0 00007ffb`93e69fe6     ntdll!RtlReportException+0x78
04 0000001d`433fe620 00007ffb`93e1f933     ntdll!RtlReportFatalFailure$filt$0+0x33
05 0000001d`433fe650 00007ffb`93e647af     ntdll!_C_specific_handler+0x93
06 0000001d`433fe6c0 00007ffb`93d221c7     ntdll!RtlpExecuteHandlerForException+0xf
07 0000001d`433fe6f0 00007ffb`93d96ea1     ntdll!RtlDispatchException+0x437
08 0000001d`433fee40 00007ffb`93e12265     ntdll!RtlRaiseException+0x221
09 0000001d`433ff620 00007ffb`93d09d69     ntdll!RtlReportFatalFailure+0x9
0a 0000001d`433ff670 00007ffb`93d75ba2     ntdll!RtlReportCriticalFailure+0xa9
0b 0000001d`433ff760 00007ffb`93e1bb3a     ntdll!RtlpHeapHandleError+0x12
0c 0000001d`433ff790 00007ffb`93d330fb     ntdll!RtlpHpHeapHandleError+0x7a
0d 0000001d`433ff7c0 00007ffb`93d2fdfa     ntdll!RtlpLogHeapFailure+0x4b
0e 0000001d`433ff7f0 00007ffb`92abd60c     ntdll!RtlFreeHeap+0x6da
0f 0000001d`433ff8e0 00007ffa`eb6676bb     msvcrt!free+0x1c
10 0000001d`433ff910 00007ffa`eb54ac6e     libimhex!ZN3hex3log4impl11addLogEntryESt17basic_string_viewIcSt11char_traitsIcEES5_NSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcS4_SaIcEEE+0x21b
11 0000001d`433ff9b0 00007ffa`eb58aaa8     libimhex!ZN3hex3log5debugIJRKNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEEEEEvN3fmt3v127fstringIJDpT_EE1tEDpOSD_+0xae
12 0000001d`433ffa90 00007ffa`ec5da2ff     libimhex!ZN3hex11TaskManager18createBlockingTaskERKNS_17UnlocalizedStringEySt8functionIFvvEE+0x3e8
13 0000001d`433ffba0 00007ffb`7218673c     libstdc___6!atomic_flag_test_and_set_explicit+0x2cf
14 0000001d`433ffbe0 00007ffb`92adf0ad     libwinpthread_1!pthread_create_wrapper+0x9c
15 0000001d`433ffc20 00007ffb`92adf17c     msvcrt!_callthreadstartex+0x1d
16 0000001d`433ffc50 00007ffb`9304e957     msvcrt!_threadstartex+0x7c
17 0000001d`433ffc80 00007ffb`93d87c1c     kernel32!BaseThreadInitThunk+0x17
18 0000001d`433ffcb0 00000000`00000000     ntdll!RtlUserThreadStart+0x2c
```

The exception is about a heap operation:
```
0:010> !heap
**************************************************************
*                                                            *
*                  HEAP ERROR DETECTED                       *
*                                                            *
**************************************************************

Details:

Heap address:  000001ed54e70000
Error address: 000001ed5b076040
Error type:    HEAP_FAILURE_BLOCK_NOT_BUSY
Details:       The caller performed an operation (such as a free
               or a size check) that is illegal on a free block.
Follow-up:     Check the error's stack trace to find the culprit.

Stack trace:
Stack trace at 0x00007ffb93ecc138
    00007ffb93d330fb: ntdll!RtlpLogHeapFailure+0x4b
    00007ffb93d2fdfa: ntdll!RtlFreeHeap+0x6da
    00007ffb92abd60c: msvcrt!free+0x1c
    00007ffaeb6676bb: libimhex!ZN3hex3log4impl11addLogEntryESt17basic_string_viewIcSt11char_traitsIcEES5_NSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcS4_SaIcEEE+0x21b
    00007ffaeb54ac6e: libimhex!ZN3hex3log5debugIJRKNSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEEEEEvN3fmt3v127fstringIJDpT_EE1tEDpOSD_+0xae
    00007ffaeb58aaa8: libimhex!ZN3hex11TaskManager18createBlockingTaskERKNS_17UnlocalizedStringEySt8functionIFvvEE+0x3e8
    00007ffaec5da2ff: libstdc___6!atomic_flag_test_and_set_explicit+0x2cf
    00007ffb7218673c: libwinpthread_1!pthread_create_wrapper+0x9c
    00007ffb92adf0ad: msvcrt!_callthreadstartex+0x1d
    00007ffb92adf17c: msvcrt!_threadstartex+0x7c
    00007ffb9304e957: kernel32!BaseThreadInitThunk+0x17
    00007ffb93d87c1c: ntdll!RtlUserThreadStart+0x2c
```

I analyzed the crash dump and found that it's about a double free of memory of `std::vector`.
I checked the code and found `addLogEntry` is not protected by mutex:
```cpp
    template<typename ... Args>
    [[maybe_unused]] void debug(fmt::format_string<Args...> fmt, Args && ... args) {
        if (impl::isDebugLoggingEnabled()) [[unlikely]] {
            impl::print(fg(impl::color::debug()) | fmt::emphasis::bold, "[DEBUG]", fmt, std::forward<Args>(args)...);
        } else {
            impl::addLogEntry(IMHEX_PROJECT_NAME, "[DEBUG]", fmt::format(fmt, std::forward<Args>(args)...));
        }
    }

        void addLogEntry(std::string_view project, std::string_view level, std::string message) {
            s_logEntries->emplace_back(
                project,
                level,
                std::move(message)
            );
        }
```

The `print` method is protected:
```cpp
    template<typename ... Args>
    [[maybe_unused]] void print(fmt::format_string<Args...> fmt, Args && ... args) {
        impl::lockLoggerMutex();
        ON_SCOPE_EXIT { impl::unlockLoggerMutex(); };
...
```

### How can the issue be reproduced?

Right click a file and select "open by ImHex".

### ImHex Version

1.38.1

### ImHex Build Type

- [ ] Nightly or built from sources

### Installation type

MSI

### Additional context?

_No response_
